violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were provided during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by various personal belongings in the sink basin in the back kitchen prep space. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Personal belongings were moved during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The can opener was cleaned and sanitized during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025

Various prepared foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Food items were labeled with the date of preparation during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back hallway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of various dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed from containers during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025

Allergen advisory statement is lacking on menus. Statement is visible at order point at time of inspection. Allergen advisory statement to be placed on revised menus. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (raw chicken over traw beef). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Allergen advisory statement was lacking on menus and at order point. Statement was placed at order point at time of inspection. Allergen advisory statement to be placed on revised menus. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was placed at handsink during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Raw animal foods such as chicken was observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as vegetables in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Various foods in cookline prep unit were observed held at 42-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Prep unit was observed at an ambient temperature of 54 degrees Fahrenheit. Per interview of PIC, foods were recently placed into prep unit at time of establishment opening today. Foods were placed over ice and/or into other refrigeration to blast chill. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cookline prep unit was taken out of service to hold potentially hazardous foods during time of inspection. Cookline prep unit must be repaired/ replaced prior to use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

The screen door to the outside, located in the back of kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets for 1 door prep unit lowboy were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of various equipment such as refrigeration units, prep tables, exterior of 3 bay and prep sinks, walk-in cooler shelves and speedracks have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility (especially in walk-in cooler and back prep room) have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
